description: Lord Lemos CMG CBE was appointed as Lord in Waiting (Government Whip) on 22 July 2025. Lord Lemos joined HMPPS as Lead Non-Executive Board Member on 1 May 2018 and chaired the Agency Board leaving role on 20 March 2025. He is Chair of Hofesh Shechter Dance Company. He is the author of many publications about social policy and supporting vulnerable people, including The Good Prison: Conscience, crime and punishment published in 2014. His former roles include Chair of English Heritage, Chair of London Institute of Banking and Finance, Vice-President of the British Board of Film Classification, Chair of Payments UK, the Lending Standards Board, Banking Code Standards Board, Notting Hill Housing, and the Money Advice Service; Deputy Chair and Acting Chair of the British Council; a trustee of London University and Dartington Hall Trust, an Audit Commissioner, a Civil Service Commissioner and a non-executive director of the Crown Prosecution Service.

details.body:
Lord Lemos CMG CBE was appointed as Lord in Waiting (Government Whip) on 22 July 2025.

Lord Lemos joined HMPPS as Lead Non-Executive Board Member on 1 May 2018 and chaired the Agency Board leaving role on 20 March 2025.

He is Chair of Hofesh Shechter Dance Company. He is the author of many publications about social policy and supporting vulnerable people, including The Good Prison: Conscience, crime and punishment published in 2014.

His former roles include Chair of English Heritage, Chair of London Institute of Banking and Finance, Vice-President of the British Board of Film Classification, Chair of Payments UK, the Lending Standards Board, Banking Code Standards Board, Notting Hill Housing, and the Money Advice Service; Deputy Chair and Acting Chair of the British Council; a trustee of London University and Dartington Hall Trust, an Audit Commissioner, a Civil Service Commissioner and a non-executive director of the Crown Prosecution Service.

Lord Lemos CMG CBE was appointed as Lord in Waiting (Government Whip) on 22 July 2025.

Lord Lemos joined HMPPS as Lead Non-Executive Board Member on 1 May 2018 and chaired the Agency Board leaving role on 20 March 2025.

He is Chair of Hofesh Shechter Dance Company. He is the author of many publications about social policy and supporting vulnerable people, including The Good Prison: Conscience, crime and punishment published in 2014.

His former roles include Chair of English Heritage, Chair of London Institute of Banking and Finance, Vice-President of the British Board of Film Classification, Chair of Payments UK, the Lending Standards Board, Banking Code Standards Board, Notting Hill Housing, and the Money Advice Service; Deputy Chair and Acting Chair of the British Council; a trustee of London University and Dartington Hall Trust, an Audit Commissioner, a Civil Service Commissioner and a non-executive director of the Crown Prosecution Service.

Our non-executives are senior figures from outside the agency who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to HMPPS leaders
  • support and challenge management on the agency’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

role_appointments.title: Lord Lemos CMG CBE - Non-executive Director
